title: Playtech’s Hot Start to 2026 Has Investors Turning Bullish
author: Michael Adeleke
description: Playtech is experiencing a strong start to 2026 driven by impressive growth in the Americas, particularly the USA and Mexico, following a weak 2025. Despite 2025 revenue declining 10% to €763.6 million and a pretax loss of €128.6 million, analysts from Jefferies, Citi, and Deutsche Bank have raised their price targets. The company maintains medium-term EBITDA targets of €250-300 million and expects future growth, though it faces regulatory pressures in the UK and legal challenges from competitor Evolution AB.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mentioned as a competitor that has accused Playtech of reputation-damaging campaign in US market. Neutral sentiment as the article focuses on Playtech's perspective and does not provide detailed information about Evolution's business performance or outlook.

Strong start to 2026 with impressive growth in Americas region, particularly USA and Mexico. Multiple analyst upgrades from Jefferies, Citi, and Deutsche Bank. Chairman insider buying of 57,000 shares signals management confidence. Company maintains positive medium-term guidance despite 2025 challenges. However, sentiment is cautiously positive due to need to sustain momentum and ongoing regulatory/legal issues.
